% Optimal Transport / Schrödinger Bridge fixture
% Mimics a derivation relevant to Mixed-Type Stochastic Entropic OT.
% Contains: valid steps, a weak step (unjustified regularity), and a vague step.

\section{Entropic Optimal Transport and the Schrödinger Bridge}

Let $(\mathcal{X}, \mathcal{B}(\mathcal{X}))$ and $(\mathcal{Y}, \mathcal{B}(\mathcal{Y}))$
be Polish spaces, $\mu \in \mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X})$, $\nu \in \mathcal{P}(\mathcal{Y})$,
and $\varepsilon > 0$.

\begin{definition}
  \label{def:sinkhorn_div}
  The \emph{Sinkhorn divergence} between $\mu$ and $\nu$ at regularisation
  strength $\varepsilon$ is
  \[
    S_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu)
    \coloneqq \mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu)
      - \tfrac{1}{2}\mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \mu)
      - \tfrac{1}{2}\mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\nu, \nu),
  \]
  where $\mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu) \coloneqq
  \min_{\pi \in \Pi(\mu,\nu)} \mathbb{E}_\pi[c(X,Y)] + \varepsilon \operatorname{KL}(\pi \mid \mu \otimes \nu)$.
\end{definition}

\begin{theorem}
  \label{thm:dual_sinkhorn}
  Under the above notation, the dual formulation of $\mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu)$ is
  \[
    \mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu)
    = \sup_{f, g} \left\{
      \int_\mathcal{X} f \, d\mu + \int_\mathcal{Y} g \, d\nu
      - \varepsilon \int_{\mathcal{X} \times \mathcal{Y}}
        e^{\frac{f(x) + g(y) - c(x,y)}{\varepsilon}} \, d(\mu \otimes \nu)(x,y)
      + \varepsilon
    \right\}.
  \]
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
  We apply the Fenchel--Rockafellar duality theorem to the primal problem.
  Let $F(\pi) = \varepsilon \operatorname{KL}(\pi \mid \mu \otimes \nu)$ and
  $G(\pi) = \mathbb{E}_\pi[c]$ restricted to $\Pi(\mu, \nu)$.

  Since $F$ is convex and lower semicontinuous in the weak topology on
  $\mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X} \times \mathcal{Y})$, and $G$ is bounded below
  (as $c$ is assumed lower semicontinuous and bounded below by our standing assumptions),
  the primal problem is finite and attained.

  By standard Fenchel--Rockafellar duality (see Villani, 2009, Theorem 5.10), strong duality holds
  and we obtain
  \begin{align*}
    \mathrm{OT}_\varepsilon(\mu, \nu)
    &= \sup_{(f, g) \in C_b(\mathcal{X}) \times C_b(\mathcal{Y})}
       \inf_{\pi \in \mathcal{P}(\mathcal{X} \times \mathcal{Y})}
       \left\{
         \mathbb{E}_\pi[c - f \oplus g] + \varepsilon \operatorname{KL}(\pi \mid \mu \otimes \nu)
         + \int f \, d\mu + \int g \, d\nu
       \right\}.
  \end{align*}

  The inner infimum over $\pi$ is clearly minimised by the Gibbs kernel
  $d\pi^* = e^{(f \oplus g - c)/\varepsilon} \, d(\mu \otimes \nu)$,
  which is integrable by compactness of the support.

  Substituting and simplifying (using $\int e^{(f+g-c)/\varepsilon} \, d(\mu\otimes\nu) = Z_{f,g}$),
  we obtain the stated dual formula.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}
  The condition that $c$ is bounded below can be relaxed; it follows that the result
  extends to lower semicontinuous cost functions on Polish spaces by a standard
  approximation argument.
\end{remark}
